raw_consumer_statement: A charge ( {$580.00} ) was made by XXXX XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX on my Jet Blue Mastercard account. I got an immediate alert via email at XXXXXXXX XXXX same day. I then, immediately, at XXXXXXXX XXXX ( same day ) called Jet Blue and reported the charge as fraudulent. This phone call that I made to Jet Blue shows true on my XXXX bill details which I can provide. Jet Blue canceled my card and opened a fraud investigation. On XX/XX/XXXX I received an email stating that the charge was found not fraudulent and that I am responsible for the charge. On XXXX XXXX, I called Jet Blue again and asked them to investigate the charge a second time. On XX/XX/XXXX I got a notice that the charge was once again found not fraudulent. I asked that it be investigated a 3rd time and that Jet Blue send me all of the supporting paperwork in their investigation. They claim that XXXX XXXX XXXX has provided information proving that I am the person who purchased the tickets online, claiming that my name, address, phone number was provided during checkout. I opened an investigation through XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX, but it was denied by Barkleys Bank Delaware ( Jet Blue ) on XX/XX/XXXX. I have not received any supporting evidence from Jet Blue nor XXXX XXXX XXXX. I did not make this charge. My personal information was used fraudulently online. I reported the charge immediately after I was alerted. I do not know where else to turn to get this resolved. Please help me. I do not have this kind of money to be held responsible for fraud.
